textByHash.96a72f6c9820e1aedcc8cfd862d64f2fefab4bfe: What can I help you with?
textByHash.c436d7d0204dd5f783a8389e5369dfbea63bad3d: What sort of dye would you like? Red, yellow or blue?
textByHash.dc4db0ebc10f470db0a201757ba393633eaf9f81: Black dye can be made by grinding up black mushrooms. That's dead easy. The mushrooms grow in some old caves and ruins.
textByHash.5110d37e8d3f277ce5c2c3c987179d3ff7b9ebe8: But white dye? There's no such thing!
textByHash.eeaaf77ff67ddba406d29570df85667e87823cf2: Usually you can't, but with poorly-made clothes, it's possible to remove the dye. Even then, it's a very tricky process involving a special ingredient found in the gut of the Hemenster Whitefish.
textByHash.6de93c97d57269c937a850034c08129be2c6cdf7: Yes, found only at Hemenster, oddly enough. It's the place where they have those fishing contests.
textByHash.bad4447c33a947f621eb7bfefb685ea429f50898: If you fancy catching some, you should know that very specific bait is needed. Only slimy eels will work. I hear you can catch those in the caves below Lumbridge Swamp.
textByHash.f2bd4c5b495f8ca515fff127e0c03bfc53508c6c: Anyway, if you do have something you want the dye removing from, just bring me a Hemenster Whitefish and five coins.
textByHash.70cc6f4e329b3bb9129d2ff776684e070de293d0: You'll need to go and get a Hemenster Whitefish first.
textByHash.d326ead20270286e0c5a0039d83527d74f5ae7c7: That goblin mail is already white.
textByHash.aa70f04c63f59c681d7db9ecfd26a450550d4896: Not at the minute I'm afraid...I'm really worried! My new apprentice 'Jimmy the Chisel' has gone missing!
textByHash.6d093d003ffbc6dbf5c136ffb816f1d83c2cfcfa: It's sort of like a 'street name'...it's a new fad ...perhaps you've seen it around? Anyway, it's not his name I'm concerned with, it's his whereabouts that worries me!
textByHash.c5a92ca02daa77b0c815d340e42ca914577eee93: Well, I sent him to Lumbridge to get some supplies and he hasn't come back! He left about two days ago and I've seen neither hide nor hair of him all that time.
textByHash.27f1907169991d17fdaaaa703df66b946fcbc85e: Not really, I mean, not for definite. But I did hear old Ned talking about some strange activity in that ruined building infested with goblins and spiders in between here and Lumbridge.
textByHash.7aa8c37c000e5cea6a8b7b7add4f1154858b013f: It's probably nothing...but it would aid my mind a lot to know he was safe. I can't leave here until he returns. What if he's had an accident and needs some emergency magical attention?
textByHash.61040e2c4ef95568cba5f768caa89801926c2a01: Would you my dear, it would only be a small favour...one small favour, that's all I ask.
textByHash.2a05f0714f0b002795f75bad813b839dc135552b: Could you go on and check out that abandoned building and see if there's anything odd going on. Just check to see if there's any sign of young Jimmy.
textByHash.ae3f23f182d2a2d0e1f3ca824169bb1abe80e604: Why you big meanie! He could be dead for all you know!
textByHash.16a09aab645d84c436b33bd33c09ee8c5f90d6df: Oh, thanks ever so much...I do appreciate it! Good Luck!
textByHash.e700a2026d0845ede908d24aea3bba868b16564d: Well, I had hoped that you would go and look for 'Jimmy the Chisel'...but it looks like you've had other 'more important' things to do!
textByHash.bc5423af773068fde04468e6e9bd2aab0a8c7038: I don't know where he is! But I suggest that you check out that ruined building to the east, you know, the one with all the spiders in it! Doesn't look like anyone's been living in there for years!
textByHash.b8d61ff7a6fa5457ac9a8e2b7ae1ece5726333d0: Oh, I don't know, you mumbled something about 'witnesses', I just can't think straight worrying so much about that poor little Jimmy.
textByHash.ddce0666c676f633c7de41f45d3b8189a9779b27: He could be lying somewhere in a pool of his blood, crying out for help...oooh, it's too much for me to bear.
textByHash.8ceb0fd9a304df41fbcd3f8c071fd13387269838: Yes! He's just returned! I'll happily be a character witness for Brian now! Please let him know that I'll help his friend however I can.
textByHash.47916842a0231a5012cabddaa60db11e4828206b: Why, it's one of my most popular potions. The women here, they like to have smooth looking skin. And I must admit, some of the men buy it as well.
textByHash.b25bc808743ffb50b086917cf9eeba743e2f27d4: I can make it for you, just get me what's needed.
textByHash.51ac30e4d35c28e82abb8414305d5f049740e85d: Well dearie, you need a base for the paste. That's a mix of ash, flour and water. Then you need redberries to colour it as you want. Bring me those four items and I will make you some.
textByHash.1f8053b8d21e680288b10b2f60d96ee0d4c7e201: Yes I can, I see you already have the ingredients. Would you like me to mix some for you now?
textByHash.be8f35ae53996faf7fdd3ca54747407c59e6f12b: That should be simple. Hand the things to Aggie then.
textByHash.2fd0d9da87f9406f2e52fcfdd838318fabcb979c: There you go dearie, your skin potion. That will make you look good at the Varrock dances.
textByHash.37d57c194ea8ada5262a4fc78e92c94dc31085c1: Okay dearie, that's always your choice.
textByHash.e4ccb84fb660fd90a6e4304410ebea30740f622b: Why, it's one of my most popular potions! Lots of people around here like to pretty their faces up a bit. I can make it for you if you get me what's needed.
textByHash.d5c3f718c7f1c8d04772b14592fae783234dfb83: Yes I can. I see you already have the ingredients. Would you like me to mix some for you now?
textByHash.02fa40895982aa734c4992b7a94da38544de8a4b: There you go dearie. That will make you look good at the Varrock dances.
textByHash.0dbfc8904c518a5ca535c60e57d60b0edc98d657: I mostly just make what I find pretty. I sometimes make dye for the women's clothes to brighten the place up. I can make red, yellow and blue dyes. If you'd like some, just bring me the appropriate ingredients.
textByHash.88139e6aa50c327e02795f355fc2030663b05189: 3 lots of redberries and 5 coins to you.
textByHash.82062ab880837d4548e4757c6c952de9f1bd0017: You know what you need to get, now come back when you have them. Goodbye for now.
textByHash.3135f19fc1c9f247c08d9ef6fc22efb8cbb2cd3b: That's your choice, but I would think you have killed for less. I can see it in your eyes.
textByHash.85bef8fb1a92f50a50daf7791ab1019402cde068: I pick mine from the woods south of Varrock. The food shop in Port Sarim sometimes has some as well.
textByHash.e1304359c4fca6a2f0762e386ff23e0bcc3e2496: Red, yellow and blue. Which one would you like?
textByHash.d7657fb26db7d8f1e0b9b766c813e7a4ce9a5099: Yellow is a strange colour to get, comes from onion skins. I need 2 onions and 5 coins to make yellow dye.
textByHash.8637b9cee2aadfa6d5d2369f5fde127aaa8d3f9d: There are some onions growing on a farm to the East of here, next to the sheep field.
textByHash.83a381413205053669a61994f8b78a1df457b29d: 2 woad leaves and 5 coins to you.
textByHash.1e8c62fe2bcd20920c22461ee8946e665f63f64e: Woad leaves are fairly hard to find. My other customers tell me the chief gardener in Falador grows them.
textByHash.7f82e9abe369144f59fe31b5280f7b759db723dc: You are easily pleased with yourself then. When you need dyes, come to me.
textByHash.99863bec08224e94a128709aa12b06d1fbe47cc3: Oh, not for years, but if you meet a talking chicken, you have probably met the professor in the manor north of here. A few years ago it was flying fish. That machine is a menace.
textByHash.e27f3f6b1dee91888065a0387c74ef6694fe7ac4: Oh, you like to call a witch names do you?
textByHash.75211ef90ef983d168f7719f8a2a6fdb3d5d8a31: That's a fine for insulting a witch. You should learn some respect.
textByHash.0e228d35c933a165149672572caca00720b3a400: Thank you for your kind present of some flour. I am sure you never meant to insult me.
textByHash.b29aafbf88f6cbd8a8a66ca38e705f610f4475a5: You should be careful about insulting a witch. You never know what shape you could wake up in.
